themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has triggered intense cross-national reactions, with right-wing media framing it as an attack on democracy and a symptom of escalating political violence. Coverage spans the U.S., Europe, and beyond, with shared narratives of security failures and partisan blame.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense focus on security lapses, shooter's motives, and calls for heightened protection for Trump and allies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemns the attack as political violence, emphasizing solidarity with Trump.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Bardella and Le Pen frame the incident as evidence of rising political extremism in the U.S.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage links the shooting to broader concerns about political polarization and media bias.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal condemns the attack as an assault on democratic values, tying it to anti-Trump rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD uses the incident to criticize U.S. political instability and media narratives.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS condemns the attack as an assault on democracy, calling for international solidarity with Trump.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a polling surge, with shared narratives of anti-immigration, economic nationalism, and opposition to EU fiscal rules. This trend is evident in Germany, France, Spain, Italy, and Scandinavia, where parties like AfD, RN, Vox, and Sverigedemokraterna are gaining ground.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads in multiple state polls, with 28-38% support, positioning itself as Germany's strongest party.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Bardella and Le Pen maintain strong polling leads ahead of the 2027 presidential election.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox gains traction in regional elections, particularly in Andalusia and Aragón, despite internal conflicts.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD consolidates its position as Sweden's second-largest party, influencing government policies.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP gains support on anti-immigration and economic liberalization platforms.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's party polls strongly in local elections, challenging Labour and Conservatives.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's FdI and Salvini's Lega maintain strong polling despite recent declines.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalition governments between Spain's Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regions like Aragón, Extremadura, and Castilla y León has ignited cross-national debates over constitutional legality, national identity, and the prioritization of Spanish citizens in public services. This theme resonates in Italy and Poland, where similar policy debates are unfolding.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox pushes 'national priority' policies in coalition deals, sparking legal and political controversies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP negotiates with Vox, adopting far-right policies to secure regional governments, despite internal dissent.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN monitors Vox's coalition strategies, drawing parallels to its own policy proposals.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government debates similar 'national priority' policies, particularly in immigration and welfare.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS debates constitutional reforms that echo Spain's 'national priority' debates, particularly in healthcare and immigration.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán's decision to step down as Hungary's prime minister and not take his parliamentary seat has triggered a cross-national discussion about the future of right-wing populism in Central Europe. This theme is closely watched in Poland, Germany, and France, where similar leadership transitions are anticipated.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán's resignation and party reshuffle dominate coverage, with debates over his successor and Fidesz's future.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS monitors Orbán's exit, drawing parallels to Kaczyński's leadership challenges and potential succession.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD analyzes Orbán's legacy, framing it as a cautionary tale for right-wing populist movements.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU officials react to Orbán's resignation, anticipating shifts in Hungary's stance on EU funds and migration.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN draws lessons from Orbán's exit, particularly regarding EU relations and internal party dynamics.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: The rise of far-right rhetoric in political discourse is increasingly linked to incidents of political violence, with media in Italy, Spain, the UK, and the U.S. highlighting this connection. This theme explores the transnational spread of violent rhetoric and its real-world consequences.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemns political violence, particularly during April 25 protests, linking it to far-right rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox faces accusations of fueling political violence, particularly in regional elections and protests.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's party is scrutinized for candidates' extremist views and ties to violent rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric is linked to political violence, including the WHCD shooting and other incidents.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN faces criticism for its rhetoric, particularly regarding immigration and national identity.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS debates the impact of its rhetoric on political polarization and violence in Poland.
